title: Adding Providers
summary: Comprehensive guide for adding a new inference provider to Hermes, covering two paths (OpenAI-compatible plugin vs. native provider), file checklists, auth.py, models.py, runtime_provider.py, main.py wiring, auxiliary client defaults, and common pitfalls.

cli_commands.purpose: Verify a new provider appears in the interactive model selection
Verify the provider appears in the setup wizard

code_examples.caption: Minimal model-provider plugin
code_examples.code:
# plugins/model-providers/acme/__init__.py
from providers import register_provider
from providers.base import ProviderProfile

register_provider(ProviderProfile(
    name="acme-inference",
    aliases=("acme",),
    env_vars=("ACME_API_KEY",),
    base_url="https://api.acme.example.com/v1",
    auth_type="api_key",
))

code_examples.caption: Live smoke test
code_examples.code:
source venv/bin/activate
python -m hermes_cli.main chat -q "Say hello" --provider your-provider --model your-model

gotchas: Adding provider to auth.py but not model parsing causes credentials to work while /model and provider:model inputs fail
config["model"] can be a string or a dict — normalize both forms
Never assume a built-in provider is required — if service is just OpenAI-compatible, custom provider may suffice
Forgetting auxiliary paths means summarization, memory flushes, or vision helpers fail silently
hermes_cli/setup.py does NOT need changes — it delegates to select_provider_and_model() in main.py
full_text:
# Adding Providers

Hermes can already talk to any OpenAI-compatible endpoint through the custom provider path. Do not add a built-in provider unless you want first-class UX for that service:
- provider-specific auth or token refresh
- a curated model catalog
- setup / `hermes model` menu entries
- provider aliases for `provider:model` syntax
- a non-OpenAI API shape that needs an adapter

## Fast path: Simple API-key providers

All you need is a plugin directory under `plugins/model-providers/<your-provider>/` containing:
- `__init__.py` — calls `register_provider(profile)` at module-level
- `plugin.yaml` — manifest (name, kind: model-provider, version, description)

When you add a plugin and it calls `register_provider()`, the following wire up automatically:
1. `PROVIDER_REGISTRY` entry in `auth.py`
2. `api_mode` set to `chat_completions`
3. `base_url` sourced from the config or the declared env var
4. `env_vars` checked in priority order for the API key
5. `fallback_models` list registered
6. `--provider` CLI flag accepts the provider id
7. `hermes model` menu includes the provider
8. `hermes setup` wizard delegates automatically
9. `provider:model` alias syntax works
10. Runtime resolver returns correct `base_url` and `api_key`
11. `HERMES_INFERENCE_PROVIDER` env-var override accepts the provider id
12. Fallback model activation can switch into the provider cleanly

## Full path: OAuth and complex providers

Use the full checklist below when your provider needs:
- OAuth or token refresh
- A non-OpenAI API shape that requires a new adapter
- Custom endpoint detection or multi-region probing
- A curated static model catalog or live `/models` fetch

### File checklist (Required for every built-in provider)

1. `hermes_cli/auth.py`
2. `hermes_cli/models.py`
3. `hermes_cli/runtime_provider.py`
4. `hermes_cli/main.py`
5. `agent/auxiliary_client.py`
6. `agent/model_metadata.py`
7. tests
8. user-facing docs under `website/docs/`

### Additional for native / non-OpenAI providers

9. `agent/<provider>_adapter.py`
10. `run_agent.py`
11. `pyproject.toml` if a provider SDK is required

## Common pitfalls

1. Adding the provider to auth but not to model parsing
2. Forgetting that `config["model"]` can be a string or a dict
3. Assuming a built-in provider is required (maybe a custom provider suffices)
4. Forgetting auxiliary paths (summarization, memory flushes, vision)
5. Native-provider branches hiding in `run_agent.py` — search for `api_mode` and `self.client.`
6. Sending OpenRouter-only knobs to other providers
7. Updating `hermes model` but not `hermes setup`

## Good search targets while implementing

- `PROVIDER_REGISTRY`, `_PROVIDER_ALIASES`, `_PROVIDER_MODELS`
- `resolve_runtime_provider`, `_model_flow_`, `select_provider_and_model`
- `api_mode`, `_API_KEY_PROVIDER_AUX_MODELS`, `self.client.`
